title: Refactor OCP-012 (OCP): The 'print' method contains a large switch statement dispatching on 'result.g...

detected_issue.description: The 'print' method contains a large switch statement dispatching on 'result.getResultType()'.
detected_issue.reasoning: Adding a new type of assertion result (new 'ResultWas::OfType' enum value) would require modifying this switch statement to add a new case, violating the Open/Closed Principle. This behavior should ideally be extended through polymorphism or a strategy pattern, where each result type knows how to print itself or has a dedicated printer strategy.

fix_logic.summary: Refactor TapAssertionPrinter to use a dispatch map for result types to improve OCP compliance.
fix_logic.rationale: The large switch statement in TapAssertionPrinter is a classic OCP violation. By introducing a dispatch map (or a series of handler methods), we decouple the printer logic from the specific enum values, allowing for easier extension without modifying the core print loop.
